scores.materiality_reasoning: Wage/salary (remuneration) is a tangible monetary resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically concerns compensation for holding a particular public office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Pay is tied to the duration of the term of office.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Magistrates hold a social/professional role acquired through selection or election.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the temporary measures of the Coup of 411 BCE, roughly 80 years before Aristotle wrote.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports a specific historical constitutional decree as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a magistrate does not inherently include or exclude the right to pay.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political decision-making power has direct material effects on property and treaties.
scores.generality_reasoning: Full powers of state administration is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The franchise is a long-term status, though here limited to the 'war period'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by property/wealth bracket (pecuniary capability).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the revolutionary government of 411 BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the constitutional arrangement of the 5000 as a matter of record.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Ability to serve does not tautologically imply the legal right to rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for a council is access to a social role with material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for a primary governing body is a broad political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is bounded by the life stage of being under thirty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed status at any given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a historical constitutional proposal from the late 5th century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific age requirement of the proposal as fixed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor (under 30 here) does not tautologically define council exclusion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The 'franchise' includes the right to vote and hold power, affecting material law.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to vote/franchise is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizen franchise is a long-term/lifelong status for those qualifying.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by possession of military equipment (wealth bracket).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the transition of power in 411 BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Aristotle adds personal evaluation ('admirable') but reports the restriction as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Property ownership is not conceptually the same as the right to vote.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct monetary payment (pay) for work performed.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically concerned with the category of public office wages.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The abolition is presented as a structural legal change for the regime's duration.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'poor' is a description of a wealth bracket/social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a historical vote during the 5th century oligarchic coups.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim records a specific legislative act ('they voted').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being poor is defined by lack of money, but not by the specific legal right to state pay.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Control over inheritance (testamentary freedom) has direct material wealth consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the legal procedure of challenging a will.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inheritance rights are a lifelong structural feature of family status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is a biologically/socially fixed immutable trait.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes legal changes made by the Thirty Tyrants (404 BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the repeal of specific legal clauses as a historical event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman does not tautologically mean lacking influence over a will.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from execution concerns bodily survival and integrity.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bodily integrity and protection from death is a foundational right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Protection of life is a permanent/lifelong requirement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status of 'eminent for birth' is a hereditary, birth-fixed trait.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the actions of the Thirty Tyrants (historical).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the pattern of executions as observed historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Possessing wealth does not tautologically entail being executed.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Absence of protection from summary execution is a direct threat to life.
scores.generality_reasoning: Due process/protection from death is a foundational societal right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Legal standing and right to life are lifelong/structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/exclusion from a formal list (Three Thousand) is hard-to-change legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to specific laws of the Thirty (404 BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Aristotle cites the specific content of two laws passed by the Council.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define whether one is on a specific sub-list.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise is a legal right with direct material effect on power.
scores.generality_reasoning: The franchise is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Disqualification is a long-term/permanent legal status change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined by a specific past action (demolition of a fort).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a historical decree of the Thirty.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the passage of a disqualification law as factual history.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Participating in a protest does not define lack of a franchise.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to bear arms involves physical goods (weapons) and bodily defense.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bearing arms is a major category of civic right/status.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Disarmament is a long-term status for the non-enrolled citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Excluded group is the general citizen body minus the 3000 list.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical actions of the Thirty Tyrants.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the physical act of disarmament as an attested event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not tautologically define whether one owns a sword.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Independent powers of government and property enjoyment have clear material effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Self-government is a broad category of political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This was established as a permanent settlement arrangement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Residency/settler status in a specific town (Eleusis) is a durable legal identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part of the reconciliation terms after the fall of the Thirty.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific terms of a formal treaty/peace agreement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a resident of Eleusis does not define having political independence.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for office is a concrete legal right with salary/power implications.
scores.generality_reasoning: Holding office is a major recognizable right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restriction holds for the duration of residency at Eleusis.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Residency in a specific seceded polity is a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the post-403 BCE reconciliation protocol.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific clause of the reconciliation agreement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Living elsewhere does not definitionally bar one from office in the home city.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise (citizenship) is a foundational status with material legal effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: The franchise is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Slavery and citizenship are lifelong, structural legal statuses.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status, requiring manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a specific prosecution of Thrasybulus (historical).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Uses a factual historical prosecution as evidence of the legal norm.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery is unfreedom, but not definitionally 'exclusion from voting' in all contexts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Attendance fees are direct cash payments (three obols a day).
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the activity of Assembly attendance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The provision was a permanent shift in Athenian practice until revoked.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal/structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the standard practice in force during Aristotle's own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Straightforward reporting of the history and current state of the fees.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not definitionally include being paid to meet.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise determines legal personhood and access to all state resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis defining the entire political class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship is a lifelong structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined by lineage (birth from both parents).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary Athenian law on citizenship.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the legal criteria for enrolment as objective fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Birth to citizens is the criterion, not the definition of the franchise itself.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Tax exemption (metoikion or liturgies) is a direct financial benefit.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad exemption from all taxes across two years.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Limited to the two-year period of military training.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (18-20) is biologically fixed at the time of service.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary system of the Athenian Ephebeia.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes current legal statues regarding military service exemptions.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a youth/trainee does not tautologically mean one is tax-exempt.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from litigation affects financial risk and legal personhood.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the context of avoiding legal distractions during service.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Limited to the specific two-year life stage of garrison duty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically determined group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary procedures for Athenian ephebes.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports legal procedure and its exceptions as plain fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a soldier or 18 doesn't definitionally exclude one from the courts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to speak is access to a practice that can produce material outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to address the sovereign body is a major political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A recurring right for any citizen throughout their adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes current Athenian assembly procedural norms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes procedural rules of the Assembly session.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not definitionally include the right to present petitions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from execution directly concerns bodily survival.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational right to due process/trial in capital cases.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Permanent structural legal protection for the citizenry.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal/structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a law established in the past that remains in force.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the passage of a specific law and its outcome.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen is not definitionally the same as being immune to council execution.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to prosecute officials has significant legal/financial consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad legal right to challenge executive authority.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong right held as part of the citizen package.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Individuals as citizens constitute a stable legal group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Athenian legal mechanisms for accountability.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the current legal standing of individuals to lay information.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A citizen is not defined by the act of laying an information.
